toolUseCases.claude: Úselo cuando desee flujos de trabajo de planificación sólidos y refactorizaciones largas de varios archivos con Claude Code.
toolUseCases.codex: Úselo cuando su equipo esté estandarizado en los flujos CLI de OpenAI Codex y la autenticación basada en perfiles.
toolUseCases.droid: Úselo cuando necesite un agente terminal liviano enfocado en codificación rápida y bucles de ejecución de comandos.
toolUseCases.openclaw: Úselo cuando desee un agente de codificación estilo Open Claw pero enrutado a través de políticas de OmniRoute.
toolUseCases.cline: Úselo cuando configure agentes de codificación dentro de editores y desee una configuración guiada con modelos OmniRoute.
toolUseCases.kilo: Úselo cuando su flujo de trabajo dependa de los comandos de Kilo Code y de ediciones iterativas rápidas.
toolUseCases.cursor: Úselo cuando codifique en Cursor y necesite modelos personalizados compatibles con OpenAI a través de OmniRoute.
toolUseCases.continue: Úselo cuando ejecute Continuar en IDE y necesite una configuración de proveedor portátil basada en JSON.
toolUseCases.opencode: Úselo cuando prefiera ejecuciones de agentes nativos de terminal y automatización con scripts a través de OpenCode.
toolUseCases.kiro: Utilícelo al integrar Kiro y controlar el enrutamiento de modelos de forma centralizada desde OmniRoute.
toolUseCases.windsurf: Use when you want an AI-first IDE with Codeium/Windsurf models routed through OmniRoute.
toolUseCases.antigravity: Úselo cuando el tráfico de Antigravity/Kiro debe interceptarse a través de MITM y enrutarse a OmniRoute.
toolUseCases.copilot: Úselo cuando desee una experiencia de usuario estilo chat Copilot mientras aplica las claves de OmniRoute y las reglas de enrutamiento.
toolUseCases.amp: Use when you want Amp CLI routed through OmniRoute with stable shorthand aliases and OpenAI-compatible setup.
toolUseCases.hermes: Úselo cuando necesite un asistente de IA nativo de terminal liviano para tareas rápidas.
toolUseCases.hermes-agent: Utilizar cuando necesites el agente Hermes (de Nousresearch) con modelos predeterminados, de delegación, visión y auxiliares enrutados mediante OmniRoute.
toolUseCases.custom: Úselo para implementaciones de herramientas personalizadas o configuraciones genéricas compatibles con OpenAI.

combos.title: combos
combos.description: Cree combinaciones de modelos con enrutamiento ponderado y soporte alternativo
combos.autoCatalogDescription: Las combinaciones automáticas/* integradas se resuelven dinámicamente desde proveedores conectados. Utilice cualquiera de estos ID como campo modelo; no es necesaria ninguna configuración.
combos.autoCatalogExpand: Ampliar el catálogo de enrutamiento automático
combos.noModelsYet: Aún no se han agregado modelos
combos.failedCreate: No se pudo crear el combo
combos.failedUpdate: No se pudo actualizar el combo
combos.failedToggle: No se pudo alternar el combo
combos.priorityDesc: Retroceso secuencial: prueba primero el modelo 1, luego el 2, etc.
combos.weightedDesc: Distribuye el tráfico por porcentaje de peso con respaldo
combos.roundRobinDesc: Distribución circular: cada solicitud pasa al siguiente modelo en rotación
combos.contextRelayDesc: Preserves session continuity with handoff summaries when accounts rotate
combos.randomDesc: Selección aleatoria uniforme y luego recurrir a los modelos restantes.
combos.leastUsedDesc: Elige el modelo con menos solicitudes y equilibra la carga a lo largo del tiempo.
combos.costOptimizedDesc: Rutas al modelo más barato primero según el precio
combos.cacheOptimizedDesc: Routes each reusable prompt prefix consistently to the same provider account
combos.resetAwareDesc: Equilibra la cuota restante con los restablecimientos semanales y de 5 h, y luego realiza todos contra todos puntuaciones similares
combos.strictRandomDesc: Shuffle deck — uses each model once before reshuffling
combos.fusionDesc: Fans the prompt to every panel model in parallel, then a judge model synthesizes one final answer
combos.fusionJudgeModelHelp: Model that synthesizes the panel answers into one final response. Leave empty to use the first panel model.
combos.fusionMinPanelHelp: Successful panel answers required before stragglers get a grace window (default 2).
combos.fusionStragglerGraceMsHelp: How long to wait for slow panel models once quorum is reached (default 8000).
combos.fusionPanelHardTimeoutMsHelp: Absolute cap so one hung model can't stall the whole panel (default 90000).
combos.contextRelayHandoffThresholdHelp: When quota usage reaches this threshold, OmniRoute generates a structured handoff summary before the active account is exhausted.
combos.contextRelayMaxMessagesHelp: Limits how much recent history is condensed into the relay summary.
combos.contextRelaySummaryModelHelp: Optional override model used only for generating the handoff summary. Leave empty to reuse the active combo model.
combos.contextRelayProviderNote: Context Relay currently generates handoffs for Codex account rotation. Pair it with multiple accounts of the same provider for the best continuity.
combos.advancedHint: Déjelo vacío para usar los valores predeterminados globales. Estos anulan la configuración por proveedor.
combos.failoverBeforeRetry: Conmutación por error antes de reintentar
combos.liveFleetDataHint: Fleet data arrives through live combo events.
combos.liveDataHint: Live data arrives through the WebSocket combo channel.
combos.liveDisconnected: Live disabled — WebSocket disconnected. Showing last known state.
combos.playgroundDescription: Simulate how requests will be routed through your combos
combos.playgroundEmptyHint: Select a combo and click Simulate Route to see the routing path.

priority.when: Tiene un modelo preferido y sólo desea recurrir al fracaso.
priority.avoid: Necesita distribución de solicitudes entre modelos.
priority.example: Modelo de codificación primaria con respaldo más económico para cortes.

weighted.when: Necesita tráfico controlado dividido entre modelos.
weighted.avoid: No se pueden mantener pesos precisos a lo largo del tiempo.
weighted.example: 80% de modelo estable + 20% de implementación de modelo canario.

round-robin.when: Quiere una distribución predecible y uniforme.
round-robin.avoid: Los modelos difieren demasiado en latencia o costo.
round-robin.example: Mismo modelo en varias cuentas para distribuir el rendimiento.

random.when: Quiere una distribución sencilla con una configuración mínima.
random.example: Creación rápida de prototipos con modelos equivalentes.

least-used.when: Quiere un equilibrio adaptativo basado en la demanda en vivo.
least-used.avoid: El tráfico es demasiado bajo para beneficiarse del equilibrio de uso.
least-used.example: Cargas de trabajo mixtas en las que un modelo a menudo se sobrecarga.

cost-optimized.when: La reducción de costos es su principal prioridad.
cost-optimized.avoid: Faltan datos de precios o están desactualizados.
cost-optimized.example: Trabajos en segundo plano o por lotes donde se prefiere un menor costo.

reset-aware.when: Se enruta a través de varias cuentas con telemetría de cuotas y diferentes ventanas de restablecimiento.
reset-aware.avoid: La telemetría de cuotas no está disponible para la mayoría de las cuentas.
reset-aware.example: Prefiera un restablecimiento semanal del 60 % de la cuenta mañana a un restablecimiento del 80 % más tarde.

strict-random.when: Use when you want perfectly even spread — each model used once before repeating.
strict-random.avoid: Avoid when models have different quality or latency and order matters.
strict-random.example: Example: Multiple accounts of the same model to distribute usage evenly.

p2c.when: Use when you want low-latency selection using Power-of-Two-Choices algorithm.
p2c.avoid: Avoid for small combos with 2 or fewer models — no benefit over round-robin.
p2c.example: Example: High-throughput inference across 4+ equivalent model endpoints.

context-relay.when: Use when long sessions must survive account rotation without losing the working context.
context-relay.avoid: Avoid when account switching is rare or when you do not want extra summarization requests.
context-relay.example: Example: Codex sessions that rotate across multiple accounts near quota exhaustion.

fill-first.when: Úselo cuando desee agotar por completo la cuota de un proveedor antes de pasar al siguiente.
fill-first.avoid: Evite cuando necesite equilibrio de carga a nivel de solicitud entre proveedores.
fill-first.example: Ejemplo: utilice todos los créditos de Deepgram de $200 antes de recurrir a Groq.

auto.when: Úselo cuando necesite un enrutamiento de puntuación multifactor basado en el costo, la latencia y la calidad.
auto.avoid: Evítelo cuando necesite un orden de prioridad estricto o persistencia histórica.
auto.example: Ejemplo: solicitudes de equilibrio entre modelos con diferentes puntos fuertes.

lkgp.when: Úselo cuando desee enrutar según las tasas históricas de éxito y el rendimiento.
lkgp.avoid: Evite cuando los datos históricos sean limitados o poco confiables.
lkgp.example: Ejemplo: ruta hacia modelos con un buen historial en tareas específicas.

context-optimized.when: Úselo cuando necesite optimizar el uso de la ventana de contexto en todos los modelos.
context-optimized.avoid: Evite cuando los modelos tengan una duración de contexto similar o las tareas sean simples.
context-optimized.example: Ejemplo: distribuir conversaciones largas entre modelos con ventanas de contexto más grandes.

advancedHelp.maxRetries: Cuántos reintentos se intentan antes de fallar una solicitud.
advancedHelp.retryDelay: Initial wait between retries. Higher values reduce burst pressure.
advancedHelp.healthcheck: Skips unhealthy models/providers from routing decisions.
advancedHelp.concurrencyPerModel: Max simultaneous requests allowed per model in round-robin.
advancedHelp.queueTimeout: How long a request can wait in queue before timing out.
advancedHelp.failoverBeforeRetry: Cuando está habilitado, cualquier error ascendente desencadena una conmutación por error inmediata al siguiente destino combinado, omitiendo todos los reintentos y las URL alternativas.
advancedHelp.maxSetRetries: Número de veces que se reintenta el conjunto de objetivos completo cuando todos los objetivos fallan. 0 = ningún reintento de nivel establecido.
advancedHelp.setRetryDelayMs: Retraso entre reintentos de nivel establecido, lo que da tiempo a los problemas transitorios para resolverse.
advancedHelp.disableSessionStickiness: Rotate to a different connection on every request instead of pinning a whole conversation to one connection by the first-message hash. Overrides the global default. Leave on Inherit to preserve prompt-cache hits for multi-turn chats.

combos.templatesDescription: Apply a starting profile, then adjust models and config.

combos.templateBalancedDesc: Rutas menos utilizadas para distribuir la demanda a lo largo del tiempo.

priority.title: Base tolerante a fallos
priority.description: Usa un modelo principal y mantén una cadena de fallback corta y confiable.
priority.tip1: Coloca primero tu modelo más confiable.
priority.tip2: Mantén 1-2 modelos de respaldo con calidad similar.
priority.tip3: Usa reintentos seguros para absorber fallos transitorios del proveedor.

weighted.title: Reparto de tráfico controlado
weighted.description: Ideal para despliegues canary y migraciones graduales entre modelos.
weighted.tip1: Empieza con una división conservadora como 90/10.
weighted.tip2: Mantén el total en 100% y autoequilibra tras cambios.
weighted.tip3: Monitorea éxito y latencia antes de subir el peso canary.

round-robin.title: Reparto de carga predecible
round-robin.description: Mejor cuando los modelos son equivalentes y necesitas distribución estable.
round-robin.tip2: Define límites de concurrencia para evitar ráfagas.
round-robin.tip3: Usa timeout de cola para fallar rápido bajo saturación.

random.title: Distribución rápida con poca configuración
random.description: Úsala cuando necesites reparto simple sin garantías estrictas.
random.tip1: Usa modelos con perfiles de latencia similares.
random.tip2: Mantén reintentos activos para absorber fallos aleatorios.
random.tip3: Prefiérela para experimentación, no para SLA estrictos.

least-used.title: Balanceo adaptativo
least-used.description: Enruta a modelos menos usados para reducir cuellos de botella con el tiempo.
least-used.tip2: Combínala con health checks para un balanceo más seguro.
least-used.tip3: Sigue el uso por modelo para validar la distribución.

cost-optimized.title: Enrutamiento orientado a costo
cost-optimized.description: Enruta a modelos más baratos cuando hay metadatos de precio disponibles.
cost-optimized.tip1: Asegura cobertura de precios para todos los modelos seleccionados.
cost-optimized.tip2: Mantén un fallback de calidad para prompts difíciles.
cost-optimized.tip3: Úsala en batch/tareas de fondo donde el costo sea el KPI principal.

reset-aware.title: Rotación de cuentas con reconocimiento de reinicio
reset-aware.description: Equilibra la cuota restante del proveedor con el tiempo de reinicio.
reset-aware.tip1: Utilice pasos de cuenta explícitos o enrutamiento de etiquetas de cuenta para proveedores con telemetría de cuotas.
reset-aware.tip2: Sesión de ajuste versus pesas semanales cuando el agotamiento a corto plazo es más riesgoso.
reset-aware.tip3: Mantenga la banda de unión pequeña para que las cuentas equivalentes sigan rotando de manera justa.

strict-random.title: Shuffle deck distribution
strict-random.description: Each model is used exactly once per cycle before reshuffling.
strict-random.tip1: Use at least 2 models for meaningful distribution.
strict-random.tip3: Ideal for load balancing across multiple API accounts.

fill-first.description: Agote las cuotas de los proveedores de forma secuencial antes de retroceder.
fill-first.tip1: Úselo para enrutamiento basado en cuotas con cadenas de respaldo claras.
fill-first.tip2: Establezca cuotas con precisión para evitar retrocesos prematuros.
fill-first.tip3: Funciona mejor con proveedores que ofrecen niveles gratuitos o grupos de crédito.
fill-first.title: Agotamiento de cuotas

auto.description: Ruta basada en puntuación en tiempo real de costo, latencia, calidad y estado.
auto.tip1: Deje que el motor equilibre múltiples factores automáticamente.
auto.tip2: Supervise qué factores impulsan las decisiones de enrutamiento en los registros.
auto.tip3: Úselo para cargas de trabajo complejas donde ningún factor domina.
auto.title: Optimización multifactor

lkgp.description: Ruta basada en patrones históricos de desempeño y éxito.
lkgp.tip1: Requiere suficiente historial de solicitudes para realizar predicciones precisas.
lkgp.tip2: Bueno para cargas de trabajo con patrones de rendimiento de modelo consistentes.
lkgp.tip3: Supervise la precisión de la predicción y ajuste las ponderaciones según sea necesario.
lkgp.title: Enrutamiento basado en historial

context-optimized.description: Optimice el enrutamiento según el uso de la ventana de contexto y la eficiencia del token.
context-optimized.tip1: Enrute conversaciones largas a modelos con ventanas de contexto más grandes.
context-optimized.tip2: Supervise la utilización del contexto para evitar el desperdicio de tokens.
context-optimized.tip3: Lo mejor para la IA conversacional que requiere una amplia retención del contexto.
context-optimized.title: Optimización del contexto

context-relay.description: Es mejor cuando se espera una rotación de cuentas y la siguiente cuenta debe heredar un resumen de tareas simplificado.
context-relay.tip1: Úselo con proveedores que rotan cuentas para la misma familia de modelos.
context-relay.tip2: Establezca el umbral de transferencia por debajo de los límites estrictos de cuota para el tiempo de generación del resumen.
context-relay.tip3: Solo establezca un modelo de resumen dedicado si el principal es demasiado caro o inestable.
context-relay.title: Prioridad de continuidad de sesión

concept.title: Conversational Memory
concept.description: OmniRoute learns from every conversation, remembering facts, episodes, procedures, and semantic concepts that make responses more accurate and context-aware.
concept.howWorksContent:
1. Automatic extraction: at the end of each response, facts and episodes are detected and saved automatically.
2. Retrieval: before each response, the most relevant memories are searched via FTS5 (exact), vector (semantic), or hybrid RRF.
3. Injection: relevant memories are injected into the assistant context to improve response quality.
4. Management: use this page to view, edit, export, and compact old memories.

providers.compatUpstreamHeadersHint: High-privilege setting — same trust level as editing provider API credentials; only trusted admins should use it. Merged after OmniRoute adds auth from the provider API key. If a custom header uses the same name as an existing one (e.g. Authorization), your value fully replaces the auto-generated header (including the Bearer token) — the upstream only sees what you typed, not the key from settings. Misconfiguration can cause 401 or broken upstream auth. One row per header (e.g. extra Authentication for some gateways). Hover or focus the value to preview. Saves on blur, outside click, or closing this panel.

providers.ideProvidersDesc: Editores con suscripción a IA incorporada. Utilice la página del proveedor para importar credenciales directamente desde el llavero IDE.
providers.noIdeProviders: Ningún proveedor IDE coincide con los filtros actuales.

providers.webFetchTooltip: Providers that extract content from web URLs (HTML → Markdown, scrape, screenshot)
providers.compatibleProvidersDesc: OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible endpoints you host or configure. Point any OpenAI SDK to your own URL and route requests here.
providers.oauthProvidersDesc: Providers authenticated via OAuth — sign in once and OmniRoute handles token rotation automatically.
providers.webCookieProvidersDesc: Estos proveedores utilizan sesiones web del navegador, cookies o tokens web en lugar de claves API. Abra un proveedor para agregar la credencial de sesión requerida.
providers.apiKeyProvidersDesc: Standard API key providers. Add your key once and OmniRoute routes, retries, and rate-limits on your behalf.
providers.noAuthProvidersDesc: Open endpoints that require no credentials — ready to use immediately without any sign-up.
providers.upstreamProxyProvidersDesc: Route outbound traffic through an upstream proxy before it reaches the provider. Useful for corporate networks or traffic inspection.
providers.webFetchProvidersDesc: Providers that fetch and extract content from web URLs. Use them to ground prompts with live web data.
providers.aggregatorsGatewaysDesc: Multi-provider aggregators and AI gateways that expose a single unified API across dozens of underlying models.
providers.enterpriseCloudDesc: Enterprise-tier and cloud-hosted models with enhanced SLAs, compliance certifications, and dedicated capacity.
providers.cloudAgentProvidersDesc: Autonomous cloud agents that execute long-running tasks with plan approval and live status tracking.
providers.localProvidersDesc: Self-hosted models running on your own hardware. No data leaves your infrastructure.
providers.searchProvidersDesc: Web and document search providers. Attach them to LLM calls for Retrieval-Augmented Generation.
providers.audioProvidersDesc: Text-to-speech and speech-to-text providers for voice I/O and audio transcription pipelines.
providers.embeddingRerankProvidersDesc: Vector embedding and reranking providers for semantic search, RAG pipelines, and similarity scoring.
providers.imageProvidersDesc: Image generation and vision providers — create images from text or analyse existing ones.
providers.videoProvidersDesc: Video generation providers. Create short video clips from text prompts or images.
providers.onboardingWizardDescription: Conecte proveedores de clave API, compatibles personalizados y OAuth con validación, persistencia y una prueba de conexión inmediata.

providers.onboardingTypeCustomText: Cree un punto final compatible con OpenAI, Anthropic o Claude Code y agregue su clave.
providers.onboardingTypeOAuthText: Reutilice los flujos de importación locales, de código de dispositivo o de OAuth existentes para los proveedores de codificación.

providers.onboardingProviderPrefixHint: Se utiliza para generar la identificación del proveedor administrado.
providers.onboardingOAuthFlowDescription: OmniRoute abrirá el flujo OAuth existente para este proveedor. Después de iniciar sesión, el asistente recarga la conexión guardada y ejecuta la misma prueba de conexión que la página del proveedor.

onboardingProviderDescriptions.agentrouter: Get $200 free credits at https://agentrouter.org/register — no credit card required.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.aimlapi: Free tier paused (2026) — AI/ML API is now pay-as-you-go only (min $20 top-up); no recurring free credits.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.ai21: $10 trial credits on signup (valid 3 months), no credit card required
onboardingProviderDescriptions.alibaba: Connect Alibaba with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.alibaba-cn: Connect Alibaba (China) with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.bailian-coding-plan: Connect Alibaba Coding Plan with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.bedrock: Native Bedrock integration: model discovery uses Bedrock foundation models and inference profiles, while chat uses the regional Bedrock Runtime Converse/ConverseStream APIs.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.anthropic: Connect Anthropic with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.ant-ling: Register and create an API key at the Ant Ling API console (https://chat.ant-ling.com/open), then paste it here. OmniRoute routes chat traffic to https://api.ant-ling.com/v1/chat/completions; the provider is OpenAI-compatible and also exposes an Anthropic-compatible surface.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.api-airforce: Get your API key from https://panel.api.airforce — OpenAI-compatible endpoint at https://api.airforce/v1
onboardingProviderDescriptions.azure-ai: Foundry uses the OpenAI v1 surface with deployment names as models. OmniRoute normalizes root resource URLs to the v1 chat and /models endpoints.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.azure-openai: Use your Azure OpenAI API key. Base URL should be your resource endpoint, for example https://my-resource.openai.azure.com.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.bai: Bearer API key for the b.ai OpenAI-compatible LLM gateway (distinct from TheB.AI). Create a key at https://docs.b.ai, then use https://api.b.ai/v1 as the OpenAI-compatible base URL.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.qianfan: Use a Qianfan API key from Baidu AI Cloud. The default endpoint is OpenAI-compatible v2.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.baseten: $30 free trial credits for GPU inference
onboardingProviderDescriptions.bazaarlink: Create a free API key at https://bazaarlink.ai — model 'auto:free' routes to zero-cost inference. All models use the provider/model-name format, e.g. xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.black-forest-labs: Connect Black Forest Labs with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.blackbox: Free tier: unlimited basic chat plus Minimax-M2.5, no credit card required
onboardingProviderDescriptions.bluesminds: Get your API key at https://www.bluesminds.com — OpenAI-compatible endpoint at https://api.bluesminds.com/v1 with free daily credits. VIP models (Claude Opus 4.5, Gemini 2.5 Pro) consume pi credits.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.byteplus: Connect BytePlus ModelArk with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.cerebras: Free Trial: 1M tokens/day, 30K TPM, 5 RPM — no credit card.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.charm-hyper: Create an API key at https://hyper.charm.land, then paste it here as a Bearer token.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.chutes: Bearer API key for the Chutes OpenAI-compatible gateway.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.clarifai: Clarifai exposes OpenAI-compatible chat, responses and /models on /v2/ext/openai/v1. Public/community models typically require a PAT; app-scoped keys only work for resources inside that app.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.cloudflare-ai: Requires API Token AND Account ID (found at dash.cloudflare.com)
onboardingProviderDescriptions.clova-studio: CLOVA Studio (HyperCLOVA X) is OpenAI-compatible on /v1/openai. OmniRoute probes /v1/openai/models and routes chat traffic to /v1/openai/chat/completions. Uses the current clovastudio.stream.ntruss.com host — the legacy clovastudio.apigw.ntruss.com endpoint is being deprecated.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.codestral: Connect Codestral with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.cohere: Free Trial: 1,000 API calls/month for testing, no credit card required
onboardingProviderDescriptions.command-code: Create or copy an API key from Command Code, then paste it here as a Bearer token.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.crof: Connect CrofAI with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.databricks: Connect Databricks with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.datarobot: The default gateway catalogs active models from /genai/llmgw/catalog/. Deployment URLs are also supported for direct OpenAI-compatible chat requests.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.deepinfra: Free signup credits for API testing and model exploration
onboardingProviderDescriptions.deepseek: 5M free tokens on signup - no credit card required
onboardingProviderDescriptions.dgrid: Create a DGrid API key at https://dgrid.ai, then use https://api.dgrid.ai/v1 as the OpenAI-compatible base URL.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.dify: Get API key from your Dify instance.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.digitalocean: Connect DigitalOcean with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.dit: dit.ai (Distributed Intelligence Trade) is an OpenAI-compatible router/gateway with dynamic per-request pricing, exposing /v1/chat/completions at https://api.dit.ai/v1. OmniRoute uses the OpenAI protocol; spend/savings analytics live in the dit.ai dashboard.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.empower: Empower exposes OpenAI-compatible chat on https://app.empower.dev/api/v1 with tool-calling support on empower-functions.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.factory: Get your Factory API key at https://app.factory.ai/settings/api-keys, then paste it as a Bearer token. OpenAI-compatible endpoint at https://api.factory.ai/v1.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.fal-ai: Connect Fal.ai with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.featherless-ai: Free tier available — no credit card required
onboardingProviderDescriptions.fenayai: Bearer API key for the FenayAI OpenAI-compatible gateway.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.firecrawl: Connect Firecrawl with an API key.
onboardingProviderDescriptions.fireworks: $1 free starter credits on signup for API testing
onboardingProviderDescriptions.freeaiapikey: Discounted API proxy for 40+ models including GPT-5, Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Qwen 3.5. Get your API key at https://freeaiapikey.com/dashboard. Base URL: https://freeaiapikey.com/v1.

onboardingProviderDescriptions.gemini: Free forever: 1,500 req/day for Gemini 2.5 Flash — no credit card, get key at aistudio.google.com
onboardingProviderDescriptions.gigachat: Connect GigaChat (Sber) with an API key.

onboardingProviderDescriptions.watsonx: The watsonx model gateway exposes OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions and /models under /ml/gateway/v1.

onboardingProviderDescriptions.poe: Poe exposes OpenAI-compatible chat and responses on https://api.poe.com/v1, with authenticated balance checks on /usage/current_balance.

settings.title: Configuración
settings.routingSettingsIntro: Controla cómo se enrutan, transforman y envían sus solicitudes a proveedores de IA.
settings.routingOpDropParagraphContainsDesc: Removes any paragraph (text block split on blank lines) inside the system prompt whose text contains ANY of the listed substrings. Use to strip third-party client fingerprints like 'github.com/anomalyco/opencode' or 'docs.openwebui.com' that Anthropic's classifier flags.
settings.routingOpDropParagraphStartsWithDesc: Removes any paragraph that STARTS WITH one of the listed prefixes. Use for identity lines like 'You are OpenCode' or 'You are Open WebUI' that announce the calling client.
settings.routingOpReplaceTextDesc: Replaces a literal substring with another literal substring. Use for known trigger phrases — e.g. rewrite 'Here is some useful information about the environment you are running in:' to 'Environment context you are running in:' (an empirically-validated trigger phrase).
settings.routingOpReplaceRegexDesc: Replaces text matching a regular expression. Use when you need patterns (character classes, optional whitespace, anchors) instead of literal substrings. A malformed pattern is caught at runtime when the op runs.
settings.routingOpDropBlockContainsDesc: Removes ENTIRE system blocks (not just paragraphs) whose text contains any of the listed substrings. Use when a whole block is fingerprint-bearing and you want it gone — e.g. an injected MCP-server description.
settings.routingOpPrependSystemBlockDesc: Inserts a new text block at the FRONT of the system array. Use to add the SDK identity 'You are a Claude agent, built on Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK.' that Anthropic's classifier expects.
settings.routingOpAppendSystemBlockDesc: Inserts a new text block at the END of the system array. Use for cosmetic additions that don't need to be at position [0].
settings.routingOpInjectBillingHeaderDesc: Prepends the special 'x-anthropic-billing-header: cc_version=...; cc_entrypoint=...; cch=...;' text block that Anthropic's classifier validates. Required for CC bridge relay endpoints; for the native claude provider OmniRoute already injects its own billing line so this op is usually redundant there.
settings.routingOpObfuscateWordsDesc: Inserts a Zero-Width-Joiner character after the first letter of each listed word, so 'opencode' becomes 'o‍pencode'. Reads identical to humans but bypasses classifier word matches. Targets system blocks, user/assistant messages, and tool descriptions.
settings.routingNeedlesHint: List of substrings. A paragraph matches if it contains ANY one of them. Add one per line via 'Add entry'.
settings.routingPrefixesHint: List of strings. A paragraph matches if it starts with any one of them (leading whitespace is trimmed before matching).
settings.routingCaseSensitiveHint: When ON, 'OpenCode' and 'opencode' are different strings. When OFF (default), the comparison ignores case.
settings.routingMatchLiteralHint: Exact literal substring to find. No regex syntax — special chars like . * ? are treated as themselves.
settings.routingReplacementTextHint: Replacement string. Leave blank to delete the match. The output preserves surrounding text.
settings.routingAllOccurrencesHint: When ON (default), every instance is replaced. When OFF, only the first match is replaced.
settings.routingPatternHint: JavaScript regex source. Don't wrap in slashes — just 'foo(.*)bar'. Server rejects patterns that fail to compile.
settings.routingRegexFlagsHint: JavaScript regex flags (g = all matches, i = case-insensitive, s = dot matches newline, m = multiline). Default 'g'.
settings.routingBlockTextHint: Full text of the new system block. Use a literal string; the system block stores text only.
settings.routingIdempotencyKeyHint: Optional. If set, the op skips when a block whose text starts with this key is already present. Prevents double-prepend on retries.
settings.routingBillingEntrypointHint: Value injected as 'cc_entrypoint='. Anthropic accepts 'sdk-cli' (Agent SDK), 'cli' (Claude Code CLI), or other documented values.
settings.routingBillingVersionFormatHint: How the 3-char build hash after cc_version= is computed. 'ex-machina' = sha256 of CCH_SALT+chars-from-first-user-msg+version (per-message). 'omniroute-daystamp' = sha256 of YYYY-MM-DD+version (stable per-day).
settings.routingBillingCchAlgoHint: How the 5-char cch= token is computed. 'sha256-first-user' = sha256 of first user message text. 'xxhash64-body' = body-level signing fills it later. 'static-zero' = literal '00000' placeholder.
settings.routingObfuscateWordsHint: Lowercase words to obfuscate. ZWJ insertion is applied case-insensitively, so 'opencode' also matches 'OpenCode' and 'OPENCODE'.
settings.routingObfuscateTargetsHint: Which body regions to scan for the words: system blocks, user/assistant messages, and/or tool descriptions.
settings.routingOpSummaryCount: {count, plural, =0 {no ops} one {# op} other {# ops}}

settings.blockedProvidersDesc: Oculte proveedores específicos de la respuesta /v1/models. Los proveedores bloqueados no aparecerán en los listados de modelos.

settings.routingClientCacheControlAlwaysDesc: Always forward client-provided cache_control headers to upstream providers as-is.
settings.routingClientCacheControlNeverDesc: Always remove client cache_control headers and let OmniRoute manage caching where native provider flows support it.

settings.resilienceEnableServerSideWait: Habilitar la espera del lado del servidor
settings.cliproxyapiImportAuthDesc: Import the OAuth accounts CLIProxyAPI already saved in ~/.cli-proxy-api/ as OmniRoute connections, so you don't have to log in to each account again. Supported account types (Gemini, Codex, Claude, Antigravity, Qwen, Kimi) are imported; others are skipped.
settings.payloadRulesDesc: Configure request payload mutations by model and protocol. Changes are persisted in settings and hot reloaded into the runtime immediately after save.
settings.payloadRuleDefaultDesc: Applies params only when the target path is missing from the outgoing payload.
settings.payloadRuleOverrideDesc: Forces values onto the payload, replacing anything already present at that path.
settings.payloadRuleFilterDesc: Removes blocked params from the payload before the upstream request is sent.
settings.payloadRuleDefaultRawDesc: Like default, but string values are parsed as JSON first when possible. The legacy input alias default-raw is also accepted on save.
settings.payloadEditorDesc: Use the runtime schema shape: default, override, filter, defaultRaw. The API also accepts the legacy input key default-raw.
settings.payloadResetInfo: Editor reset to the neutral template. Save to apply it.
settings.payloadSaveSuccess: Payload rules saved and hot reloaded.
settings.payloadMustBeObject: Payload rules must be a JSON object.
settings.payloadValidJsonRequired: Payload rules must be valid JSON before saving.
settings.requestLimitsDesc: Configure global request limits and concurrency guards.
settings.maxRequestSizeDesc: Maximum allowed size for incoming API requests.
settings.maxResponseSizeDesc: Maximum allowed size for outgoing API responses.
settings.maxRequestTokensDesc: Maximum total tokens allowed in a single request.
settings.maxResponseTokensDesc: Maximum total tokens allowed in a single response.
settings.modelCooldownsEmpty: No hay modelos en tiempo de reutilización en este momento.
settings.modelCooldownsDescription: Models temporarily isolated after a failure. When the cooldown expires they come back automatically.
settings.modelCooldownsAllReactivated: All models in cooldown have been reactivated.
settings.responsesStateHint: Auto strips previous_response_id unless a connection explicitly enables OpenAI Responses storage. Strip is safest for stateless clients such as VS Code Custom Endpoint; context then depends on the client sending full history.
settings.responsesStateSaveError: Failed to update Responses state setting
settings.codexFastTierDesc: Inyectar globalmente service_tier=priority para las solicitudes de OpenAI Codex.
settings.codexFastTierHint: Cuando está habilitado, OmniRoute añade service_tier=priority a las solicitudes salientes de Codex para las conexiones que aún no especifican un tier. El tier priority requiere una clave API de OpenAI Enterprise o la ruta de Codex con autenticación ChatGPT; otros tipos de claves recibirán un error relacionado con el tier de OpenAI. Los ajustes por conexión en la página del proveedor Codex tienen prioridad.
settings.codexFastTierSaveError: Error al actualizar el ajuste de Codex Fast Tier
settings.codexAutoPingDesc: Opt-in per connection: sends a tiny request right after a Codex session window resets so it isn't cold when you need it.
settings.codexAutoPingWarning: Consumes a small amount of real Codex quota on every ping. Off by default — enable only for connections you actively rely on.
settings.codexAutoPingSaveError: Failed to update Codex Auto-Ping setting
settings.codexFastTierModelsHint: Only checked models receive service_tier when Fast Tier is enabled.
settings.claudeFastModeDesc: Activar el Fast Mode de Anthropic (speed:"fast") en las solicitudes Claude seleccionadas.
settings.claudeFastModeHint: Anthropic no admite oficialmente Fast Mode para clientes en modo SDK. Cuando está habilitado, OmniRoute reenvía un encabezado X-CPA-Force-Fast-Mode para que una compilación compatible de CLIProxyAPI pueda reescribir el entrypoint. Solo los modelos Opus listados pasan la verificación del binario de Anthropic. La suscripción, el plan Max y el saldo de créditos Fast Mode siguen aplicándose en el servidor — Anthropic puede devolver out_of_credits incluso con el toggle activado.
settings.claudeFastModeSaveError: Error al actualizar el ajuste de Claude Fast Mode

wildcard.title: CORS is open to every origin (CORS_ALLOW_ALL=true)
wildcard.desc: Any website can call this server's API from a visitor's browser. Use only on trusted networks — set explicit origins in ALLOWED_ORIGINS and disable CORS_ALLOW_ALL in production.

prefix.empty: No prefixes configured. Bypass effectively off.

settings.resilienceRequestQueueEffect: Pone en cola las solicitudes, limita la simultaneidad y espacia las llamadas
settings.resilienceRequestQueueDesc: Esta capa solo controla las colas y el ritmo. No almacena tiempos de reutilización ni disyuntores abiertos.
settings.resilienceAutoEnableApiKeyProvidersDesc: Habilita la protección de colas de forma predeterminada para conexiones de clave API activas.
settings.resilienceMaxQueueWait: Tiempo máximo de espera en cola
settings.resilienceConnectionCooldownTrigger: Cuando una conexión devuelve un error ascendente transitorio
settings.resilienceConnectionCooldownEffect: Omite temporalmente esa conexión y aumenta la interrupción en caso de fallas repetidas
settings.resilienceConnectionCooldownDesc: El tiempo de reutilización básico cubre fallas de conexión transitorias. Cuando se habilitan las sugerencias de reintento ascendentes, la ventana explícita del proveedor anula el tiempo de reutilización local.
settings.resilienceUseUpstreamRetryHintsDesc: Utiliza valores de reintento posterior/restablecimiento del proveedor ascendente cuando están disponibles.
settings.resilienceUseUpstream429BreakerHints: Utilice sugerencias 429 ascendentes para el tiempo de reutilización del interruptor
settings.resilienceUseUpstream429BreakerHintsDesc: Aplique señales de reintento posterior/cuota agotada de 429 respuestas a la duración del enfriamiento del disyuntor. De forma predeterminada, se utiliza una política por proveedor: los proveedores de nube directos están activados de manera predeterminada; Los proveedores de proxy inverso, autohospedados y respaldados por CLI están desactivados de forma predeterminada. Independiente de "Usar sugerencias de reintento ascendentes".
settings.resilienceProviderBreakerTrigger: Errores finales de transporte/servidor después de que se agote el respaldo de la conexión
settings.resilienceProviderBreakerEffect: Bloquea temporalmente a ese proveedor hasta que expire el tiempo de reinicio
settings.resilienceProviderBreakerDesc: El estado del interruptor activo aparece solo en la página Salud. Los límites de velocidad 429 con alcance de conexión permanecen en el tiempo de reutilización de la conexión y no activan el disyuntor del proveedor.
settings.resilienceWaitForCooldownTrigger: Cuando todas las conexiones candidatas ya se estén enfriando
settings.resilienceWaitForCooldownEffect: Espera en el servidor y vuelve a intentarlo cuando expira el primer tiempo de reutilización.
settings.resilienceWaitForCooldownDesc: Esto sólo afecta a la solicitud actual. No almacena la conexión ni el estado del proveedor.
settings.resilienceEnableServerWait: Habilitar la espera del lado del servidor
settings.resilienceEnableServerWaitDesc: Cuando está habilitado, OmniRoute espera a que expire el primer tiempo de reutilización y lo vuelve a intentar automáticamente.
settings.resilienceComboCooldownWaitDesc: For quota-share combos only: wait out a short transient cooldown and re-dispatch instead of returning a 429 immediately. Never waits on quota_exhausted.
settings.resilienceComboCooldownWaitToggleDesc: Quota-share combos only; never waits on quota_exhausted.
settings.resilienceQuotaShareConcurrencyDesc: For quota-share combos only: when a connection sets a Max Concurrent cap, serialize concurrent requests to that subscription account so it is never flooded past its ceiling. Excess requests wait in the queue instead of getting a 429. The cap comes from each connection's Max Concurrent field; this switch only enables or disables honoring it.
settings.resilienceQuotaShareConcurrencyToggleDes: Quota-share combos only; honors each connection's Max Concurrent cap.
settings.resilienceProviderCooldownEffect: Skips the failed provider for a cooldown period before retrying
settings.resilienceProviderCooldownDesc: Prevents subsequent requests from re-walking the same failing providers. Cooldown scales exponentially with consecutive failures.
settings.resilienceProviderCooldownEnabledDesc: When enabled, failed providers are tracked globally and skipped for a cooldown period.
settings.forcedFingerprintTitle: Siempre activado para {provider}; necesario para la seguridad de las cuentas OAuth y no se puede desactivar.
settings.sessionAffinityDesc: Keeps one conversation on the same account for this many seconds, for any provider. 0 disables it.
settings.resetAwareQuotaCacheDesc: Almacena en caché la telemetría de cuota solo para pedidos con reconocimiento de reinicio. La verificación previa de cuotas aún protege las solicitudes. 0/0 sigue obteniendo en vivo.
settings.vercelRelayWarning: Warning: A Vercel deployment token is required. This token will only be used to create the serverless relay and will never be stored by OmniRoute.
settings.vercelRelayFreeTierNote: Relays are lightweight proxy endpoints deployed on Vercel's free tier to bypass local network/region limitations.
settings.denoRelayWarning: Warning: A Deno Deploy organization token is required. This token will only be used to create the relay app and will never be stored by OmniRoute.
settings.denoRelayFreeTierNote: Deno Deploy v2 runs on a global edge network. Free tier: 1M requests & 100GiB outbound traffic per month, no per-request CPU limits, up to 20 active apps & 50 custom domains.
settings.cloudflareRelayWarning: Deploys a Cloudflare Worker that proxies LLM requests through Cloudflare's edge network — masking the host IP behind dynamic Cloudflare addresses. The Worker enforces a one-time auth secret so the public workers.dev URL cannot be reused as an open relay.
settings.cloudflareRelayTokenHowto: Create the API token under My Profile -> API Tokens -> Create Token -> Custom Token -> Account / Workers Scripts / Edit.
settings.cloudflareRelayAccountIdHint: Found on the right side of the Cloudflare dashboard overview page.
settings.cloudflareRelayApiTokenHint: Requires 'Workers Scripts: Edit' permission. The token is used only at deploy time and is never stored.
settings.cloudflareRelayFreeTierNote: Free tier: 100,000 requests/day per Cloudflare account.
settings.cloudflareRelayCredsRequired: Account ID and API Token are required
settings.modelLockoutPageDescription: Configure which HTTP error codes trigger per-model lockout and control the cooldown behavior.
settings.modelLockoutLoadFailed: Failed to load model lockout settings
settings.modelLockoutSaveFailed: Failed to save model lockout settings
settings.modelLockoutBaseRangeError: Base Cooldown must be between 5,000ms and 600,000ms
settings.modelLockoutMaxRangeError: Max Cooldown must be between 5,000ms and 3,600,000ms
settings.modelLockoutOrderError: Max Cooldown must be greater than or equal to Base Cooldown
settings.modelLockoutStepsRangeError: Max Backoff Steps must be between 0 and 20
settings.modelLockoutEnabledDescription: When enabled, models that fail with configured error codes are temporarily locked to prevent retry loops.
settings.modelLockoutErrorCodesDescription: HTTP status codes that trigger model lockout. Type a code and click Add, or select from suggestions.
settings.modelLockoutBaseCooldownDescription: Initial cooldown duration in milliseconds before a model can be retried.
settings.modelLockoutMaxCooldownDescription: Maximum cooldown duration in milliseconds. Prevents excessively long lockouts.
settings.modelLockoutExponentialBackoffDescriptio: When enabled, each consecutive failure increases the cooldown duration exponentially.
settings.modelLockoutMaxBackoffStepsDescription: Maximum number of backoff steps before the cooldown stops growing. The Max Cooldown cap is reached first in most configurations, making this a safety ceiling for when Max Cooldown is raised.
settings.disableSessionStickinessDesc: Round-robin and random combos rotate to a different connection on every request instead of pinning a whole conversation to one connection by the first-message hash. Leave off to preserve prompt-cache hits for multi-turn chats. Per-combo overrides take precedence.
settings.promptCacheAffinityDesc: Prefer the same provider account for matching prompt-cache keys while preserving health and quota failover.
settings.credentialRedactionDesc: Redact API keys, tokens, and secrets from context sent to providers and from responses.
settings.enableCredentialRedactionDesc: Scrubs API keys, tokens, private keys, and JWTs from messages, tool calls, and responses.

headroom.name: Headroom SmartCrusher
headroom.description: Lossless tabular compaction of homogeneous JSON arrays with explicit row-count markers.

session-dedup.name: Session Dedup
session-dedup.description: Cross-turn block deduplication with recoverable content markers.

ccr.name: CCR
ccr.description: Content-addressed retrieval markers for repeated context blocks.

llmlingua.name: LLMLingua-2 (Semantic Pruning)
llmlingua.description: ONNX-based semantic token classification. Compresses prose only; code blocks and preserved constructs are protected. Fails open on model or worker errors.

lite.name: Lite
lite.description: Fast whitespace, tool-result and image URL reduction.

aggressive.name: Aggressive
aggressive.description: Summarization, tool-result compression and progressive aging.

ultra.name: Ultra
ultra.description: Heuristic token pruning with an optional local SLM fallback.

minBlockChars.label: Minimum block characters
minBlockChars.description: Minimum character count for a suffix block to be a deduplication candidate.

fuzzy.label: Fuzzy near-duplicate deduplication
fuzzy.description: Opt in to replace whole messages that are at least about 85% similar to an earlier message with a recoverable CCR marker.

minChars.label: Minimum block characters
minChars.description: Minimum character count for a block to be a CCR candidate.

retrievalRampFactor.label: Retrieval ramp factor (H8)
retrievalRampFactor.description: Controls how strongly frequently retrieved blocks resist compression. Each prior retrieval raises the effective minimum block size linearly; 1 disables the ramp.

minRows.label: Minimum rows to compact
minRows.description: Minimum rows in a homogeneous JSON array required to trigger tabular compaction. Default: 8.

contextCombos.explanationIntro: Compression reduces tokens and cost by rewriting history before it is sent to the provider while preserving meaning.
contextCombos.explanationActiveProfile: Active profile: chooses which compression profile runs globally — the panel-derived Default or one of your saved named combos.
contextCombos.explanationDefault: Default (from panel): derived from the master switch and per-engine toggles you configure in Compression Settings.
contextCombos.explanationNamedCombos: Named combos: saved pipelines you build in the named-combo editor. Selecting one makes it the active profile for every request.
contextCombos.explanationPreview: Preview: shows which engines the active profile runs, in order.
contextCombos.activeProfileDescription: Pick which compression profile runs globally — the panel-derived Default or a saved named combo.
contextCombos.contextEditingDescription: Lets the provider clear old tool-use blocks server-side, without rewriting the message.
contextCombos.contextEditingNote: Currently available for Claude (Anthropic) only. It is a delegated mode: the provider clears old tool-use blocks server-side — we do not rewrite the message. It does not affect other providers.
contextCombos.namedCombosDescription: Save different pipelines and assign them to specific routing combos.
contextCombos.nameRequired: Enter a combo name before saving.
contextCombos.pipelineRequired: Add at least one pipeline step before saving.

omniglyph.description: Context-as-image compression. Renders the system prompt, tool documentation, and dense history as compact PNG pages that Claude Fable 5 reads instead of text. Image tokens are billed by dimensions rather than characters, so the converted block costs about 10× less. Direct Anthropic route only.

economics.fewerTokens: fewer tokens on the converted block
economics.imageTokens: image tokens for a 1568×728 page (about 28k characters)

omniglyph.realRender: A real render of {characters, number} characters of dense tool documentation — not a mockup.
omniglyph.gatesDescription: Fail-closed: every gate must pass, or the request passes through untouched (each skip is recorded as skip:<reason>).

model.label: Model
model.why: Only Fable 5 reads dense pages at 100% accuracy (measured, n=30). GPT-5.5 and Gemini 2.5 Flash are blocked.

transport.label: Transport
transport.why: Aggregators resample images and destroy legibility — only the direct route is authoritative.

format.label: Format
format.why: The body must use Claude format and must never put a system role inside messages.

profitable.label: Profitable
profitable.why: The exact 28px-patch cost gate decides per request; small or sparse text passes through untouched.

omniglyph.enableDescription: Runs last in the stack (after RTK/Caveman cleans the text, OmniGlyph converts the remainder to images) and also runs standalone through omniglyph mode. This is a preview and remains off by default until end-to-end validation is complete.

translator.title: Traductor
translator.metaTitle: Patio de juegos del traductor | OmniRuta
translator.metaDescription: Depurar, probar y visualizar traducciones de formatos API entre proveedores
translator.realtime: Actividad de traducción en tiempo real
translator.modeDescriptionPlayground: Pegue cualquier cuerpo de solicitud de API y vea cómo OmniRoute lo traduce entre formatos de proveedores (OpenAI ↔ Claude ↔ Gemini ↔ API de respuestas)
translator.modeDescriptionChatTester: Envíe solicitudes de chat reales a través de OmniRoute e inspeccione el recorrido completo: entrada, solicitud traducida, respuesta del proveedor y salida traducida.
translator.modeDescriptionTestBench: Ejecute escenarios predefinidos y compare la compatibilidad entre proveedores y modelos.
translator.modeDescriptionLiveMonitor: Vea los eventos de traducción en tiempo real a medida que las solicitudes fluyen a través de OmniRoute.
translator.modeDescriptionFallback: Depure, pruebe y visualice cómo OmniRoute traduce las solicitudes de API entre proveedores.
translator.eventsAppearHint: Los eventos de traducción aparecen aquí a medida que las solicitudes fluyen a través de OmniRoute. Utilice cualquiera de estos métodos para generar eventos:
translator.inMemoryNote: Nota: Los eventos se almacenan en la memoria y se restablecen cuando se reinicia el servidor.
translator.formatConverterDescription: Pegue o escriba un cuerpo de solicitud JSON. El traductor detectará automáticamente el formato de origen y lo convertirá al formato de destino. Utilice esto para depurar cómo OmniRoute traduce solicitudes entre formatos (OpenAI ↔ Claude ↔ Gemini ↔ API de respuestas).
translator.translationPathPassthrough: Mismo formato: no se necesita traducción
translator.featureReasoningCacheDesc: Reinyecta contenido de razonamiento almacenado en caché para modelos en modo de pensamiento (DeepSeek V4, Kimi K2, Qwen) cuando los clientes lo omiten del historial de conversaciones.
translator.featureSchemaCoercionDesc: Corrige esquemas de herramientas rotos: agrega propiedades adicionales faltantes, desinfecta descripciones largas, normaliza objetos anidados.
translator.featureRoleNormalizationDesc: Desarrollador de mapas → sistema para objetivos que no son OpenAI. Sistema de mapas → usuario para modelos que no admiten la función del sistema.
translator.featureToolCallIds: Normalización de ID de llamada de herramienta
translator.featureToolCallIdsDesc: Genera ID únicos de llamada de herramienta cuando faltan. Se normaliza al formato de 9 caracteres para proveedores como Mistral.
translator.featureMissingToolResponseDesc: Inyecta mensajes tool_result vacíos cuando los clientes envían tool_calls sin las respuestas correspondientes.
translator.featureThinkingBudgetDesc: Gestiona automáticamente la configuración de pensamiento. Elimina los parámetros de pensamiento cuando el último mensaje no es del usuario.
translator.featureDirectPathsDesc: Algunos pares de formatos (Claude→Gemini) tienen traductores directos que pasan por alto el centro OpenAI, lo que produce resultados más precisos.
translator.featureImageMappingDesc: Traduce convenciones de dimensiones de imágenes entre formatos API (por ejemplo, niveles de detalle de OpenAI → dimensiones de Gemini).
translator.inputPlaceholder: Pegue el cuerpo de una solicitud aquí o seleccione una plantilla a continuación...
translator.templateLoadHint: La plantilla carga la solicitud en formato {format}. Cambie el formato de origen para cargar en un formato diferente.
translator.testBenchDescription: Ejecute escenarios predefinidos (chat simple, llamadas de herramientas, etc.) para verificar la traducción y la compatibilidad del proveedor. Seleccione un formato de origen y un proveedor de destino, luego ejecute todas las pruebas para ver un porcentaje de compatibilidad. Utilice esto para encontrar qué funciones funcionan en todos los proveedores.

landing.heroDescription: Proxy de punto final de IA con panel web: un puerto JavaScript de CLIProxyAPI. Funciona a la perfección con Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cline, RooCode y otras herramientas CLI.
landing.featuresSubtitle: Todo lo que necesita para administrar su infraestructura de IA en un solo lugar, diseñado para escalar.

docs.featureCombosText: Cree combinaciones de modelos con cadenas de respaldo y estrategias de equilibrio: round-robin, prioridad, aleatorio, menos utilizado y de costo optimizado.
docs.featureUsageText: Recuento de tokens en tiempo real, cálculo de costos por proveedor/modelo y desglose detallado del uso por clave API y cuenta.
docs.featureAnalyticsText: Análisis visual con gráficos de solicitudes, tokens, errores, latencia, costos y popularidad del modelo a lo largo del tiempo.

docs.useCaseSingleEndpointText: Dirija a los clientes a una URL base y enrútelos por prefijo de modelo (por ejemplo: gh/, cc/, kr/, openai/).

docs.clientCodexBullet2: Los modelos de la familia Codex se enrutan automáticamente a
docs.clientCodexBullet3: Los modelos que no pertenecen al Codex continúan
docs.clientCursorBullet2: Conexión OAuth: inicie sesión desde la página de Proveedores.
docs.clientWindsurfBullet1: Utilice OmniRoute como URL base compatible con OpenAI y mantenga prefijos de proveedor explícitos para un enrutamiento determinista.
docs.clientWindsurfBullet2: Apunte los modelos a `/v1/chat/completions` para el tráfico general y conserve `/v1/responses` para flujos de estilo Codex.
docs.clientWindsurfBullet3: Utilice Panel -> Herramientas CLI para obtener una guía de configuración de Windsurf lista para usar.
docs.clientClineBullet1: Cline funciona mejor con prefijos explícitos de proveedor/modelo para que el enrutador nunca tenga que adivinar el backend.
docs.clientClineBullet2: Utilice `/v1/chat/completions` para modelos generales y reutilice la misma URL base de OmniRoute en diferentes cuentas.
docs.clientClineBullet3: Utilice el panel de proveedores para validar la clave OAuth/API antes de depurar problemas de tiempo de ejecución de Cline.
docs.clientKimiBullet1: Utilice OmniRoute como URL base estable mientras rota cuentas o combinaciones de proveedores debajo.
docs.clientKimiBullet2: Prefiera modelos con prefijos en los flujos de codificación para que el respaldo y el seguimiento de auditoría sigan siendo explícitos.
docs.clientKimiBullet3: Utilice `/v1/responses` cuando desee un enrutamiento nativo estilo Respuestas para clientes que utilizan herramientas.
docs.protocolsDescription: OmniRoute expone dos protocolos operativos además de las API compatibles con OpenAI: MCP para la ejecución de herramientas y A2A para flujos de trabajo de agente a agente.
docs.protocolMcpDesc: Utilice MCP sobre stdio para permitir que los clientes descubran y llamen a las herramientas OmniRoute con visibilidad de auditoría.
docs.protocolMcpStep2: Apunte su cliente MCP al transporte stdio.
docs.protocolMcpStep3: Llame a `omniroute_get_health` y `omniroute_list_combos` para validar la conectividad.
docs.protocolA2aDesc: Utilice A2A JSON-RPC para enviar tareas de forma sincrónica o mediante transmisión SSE.
docs.protocolA2aStep3: Administre el ciclo de vida de las tareas con `tasks/get` y `tasks/cancel`.
docs.protocolTroubleshooting1: Si el estado de MCP está fuera de línea, verifique que el proceso stdio se esté ejecutando y que el archivo de latido se esté actualizando.
docs.protocolTroubleshooting2: Si las tareas A2A permanecen en `working`, inspeccione `/api/a2a/tasks/:id` y transmita eventos para ver el estado terminal.
docs.protocolTroubleshooting3: Utilice `/dashboard/mcp` y `/dashboard/a2a` para controles operativos y visibilidad de auditoría.

legal.privacyMetadataDescription: Política de privacidad para el enrutador proxy API OmniRoute AI.
legal.termsMetadataDescription: Términos de servicio para el enrutador proxy API OmniRoute AI.
legal.privacySection1Text: OmniRoute está diseñado como una aplicación local. Todo el procesamiento y almacenamiento de datos se realiza íntegramente en su máquina. No existe un servidor centralizado que recopile su información.
legal.privacyDataStoredIn: Los siguientes datos se almacenan localmente en
legal.privacyDataProviderConfigurationsDesc: URL de conexión, tipos de proveedores y configuración de prioridad
legal.privacyDataApiKeysDesc: cifrado y almacenado localmente para autenticarse con proveedores de IA
legal.privacyDataUsageLogsDesc: recuentos de solicitudes, uso de tokens, nombres de modelos, marcas de tiempo y tiempos de respuesta
legal.privacyDataApplicationSettingsDesc: preferencias de tema, estrategia de enrutamiento y configuraciones combinadas
legal.privacySection3Text: OmniRoute no recopila telemetría, análisis ni informes de fallos. No se envían datos a nosotros ni a ningún tercero. Sus patrones de uso, llamadas API y configuraciones permanecen completamente privados.
legal.privacySection4Text: Cuando realiza llamadas API a través de OmniRoute, sus solicitudes se reenvían a los proveedores de IA que haya configurado (por ejemplo: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). Estos proveedores tienen sus propias políticas de privacidad que rigen cómo manejan sus datos. Por favor revise:
legal.privacySection5Text: Si habilita la función opcional de sincronización en la nube, las configuraciones del proveedor y las claves API pueden transmitirse a un punto final en la nube configurado. Esta función está deshabilitada de forma predeterminada y requiere una suscripción explícita.
legal.privacyLoggingIntro: Los registros de solicitudes se pueden configurar a través de la configuración del panel. Puedes:
legal.privacySection7TextStart: Dado que todos los datos se almacenan localmente, usted tiene control total. Puedes eliminar tus datos en cualquier momento eliminando el
legal.privacySection7TextEnd: directorio o utilizando las funciones de copia de seguridad y restauración de la base de datos en el panel.
legal.termsSection1Text: OmniRoute es un enrutador proxy API de IA local que funciona completamente en su máquina. Enruta solicitudes a múltiples proveedores de IA con equilibrio de carga, conmutación por error y seguimiento de uso.
legal.termsResponsibilityApiKeys: Usted es el único responsable de administrar sus propias claves API y credenciales para proveedores de IA externos (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.).
legal.termsResponsibilityCompliance: Debe cumplir con los términos de servicio de cada proveedor de IA a cuya API acceda a través de OmniRoute.

cache.description: Monitor and manage semantic response cache, hit rates, and token savings.

quotaShare.conceptIntro: Quota Share divides a provider's quota among multiple API keys using work-conserving fair-share: each key receives a proportional slice but can borrow from the free balance without exceeding the global cap.
quotaShare.conceptFairShare: Fair-share: each key receives quota proportional to its configured weight
quotaShare.conceptBorrowing: Borrowing: keys can consume free balance from others without breaching the cap
quotaShare.conceptGlobalCap: Hard global cap: the provider's absolute limit is never exceeded
quotaShare.conceptWindows: Windows: 5h, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly — each tracked independently
quotaShare.conceptKeyHowDesc: Create the key normally in API Keys — it appears in the wizard's key step automatically. Tick Exclusive there to make it quota-only. No separate enable step.
quotaShare.conceptExclusiveDesc: An exclusive key only sees/uses the pool's quotaShared-* models — every other model is hidden from its /v1/models and blocked.

omni-budget.description: Configure spending limits, token quotas, and rate-limit policies per API key or globally. Inspect current consumption and enforce cost controls across providers.

omni-settings.description: Read and update global application settings: system prompts, thinking budget, IP filters, payload rules, combo defaults, and require-login configuration.

omni-cache.description: Manage the LLM response cache. View cache statistics, clear entries, configure TTL policies, and control semantic-similarity caching thresholds.

omni-compression.description: Configure RTK (command output), Caveman (prose), and stacked compression modes. Manage language packs, custom rules, and test prompt compression reducing tokens by 60–90%.

omni-context-rtk.description: Configure RTK filters, context engineering rules, and context relay settings. Test compression with real prompt samples and manage context transformation pipelines.

omni-resilience.name: Resilience & Monitoring
omni-resilience.description: Monitor provider health, circuit-breaker states, p50/p95/p99 latency metrics, and budget guard alerts. Inspect connection cooldowns and model lockouts in real time.

omni-cli-tools.description: Manage CLI tool integrations exposed via the API. List, configure, and invoke CLI tool plugins that extend OmniRoute's automation surface.

omni-tunnels.name: Tunnels
omni-tunnels.description: Create and manage secure tunnels (ngrok, Cloudflare Tunnel, custom) to expose OmniRoute to the internet or share access with remote agents and CI pipelines.

omni-sync-cloud.name: Cloud Sync
omni-sync-cloud.description: Synchronise OmniRoute configuration, provider connections, and settings to/from cloud storage. Manage cloud worker authentication and remote backup targets.

omni-db-backups.name: Database & Backups
omni-db-backups.description: Trigger system backups, restore from backup files, and manage the SQLite database lifecycle. Supports export, import, and incremental snapshot strategies.

omni-webhooks.name: Webhooks
omni-webhooks.description: Register, list, test, and remove webhook endpoints. Configure event subscriptions (request.completed, provider.error, budget.exceeded, etc.) and manage delivery retries.

omni-mcp.name: MCP Server
omni-mcp.description: Connect to the OmniRoute MCP server (37 tools, 3 transports: SSE/stdio/HTTP). Covers routing, cache, compression, memory, skills, providers, and audit tools across 16 permission scopes.

omni-agents-a2a.name: Agents & A2A Protocol
omni-agents-a2a.description: Interact with OmniRoute via JSON-RPC 2.0 agent-to-agent protocol. 6 built-in A2A skills: smart-routing, quota-management, provider-discovery, cost-analysis, health-report, list-capabilities.

omni-version-manager.name: Version Manager
omni-version-manager.description: Install, start, stop, restart, and update embedded services (9Router, CLIProxyAPI). Monitor service status, retrieve logs, and configure auto-start for local-only service endpoints.

omni-inference.name: Inference (OpenAI-compatible)
omni-inference.description: The core OpenAI-compatible inference endpoints: chat completions, embeddings, images, audio (TTS/STT), moderations, rerank, and the Responses API. The primary integration surface for AI agents.

cli-serve.name: CLI: Serve
cli-serve.description: Start, stop, and restart the OmniRoute server from the CLI. Manage daemon mode, port configuration, auto-recovery, system tray integration, and the dashboard open shortcut.

cli-health.name: CLI: Health
cli-health.description: Check server health, component status, and live metrics from the CLI. Run `health`, `health components`, and `health watch` for a real-time dashboard of circuit breakers and provider status.

cli-providers.name: CLI: Providers
cli-providers.description: Manage provider connections from the CLI: list available/configured providers, add, test, test-all, validate, rotate API keys, and view per-provider metrics.

cli-keys.name: CLI: API Keys
cli-keys.description: Create, list, rotate, and revoke OmniRoute API keys from the CLI. Manage OAuth flows for provider authentication and inspect key scopes and expiration.

cli-models.name: CLI: Models
cli-models.description: Query available AI models, list model aliases, and browse the full model catalog from the CLI. Filter by provider, search by capability, and resolve model name variants.

cli-chat.name: CLI: Chat
cli-chat.description: Send chat completions, stream responses, and start an interactive REPL session from the CLI. Supports all OmniRoute providers, combo routing, and system prompt configuration.

cli-routing.name: CLI: Routing & Combos
cli-routing.description: Create, list, update, and delete routing combos from the CLI. Test routing strategies, inspect combo metrics, and configure fallback chains interactively.

cli-resilience.name: CLI: Resilience & Quotas
cli-resilience.description: Inspect and manage circuit-breaker states, connection cooldowns, quota limits, and backoff levels from the CLI. Reset stuck providers and configure resilience thresholds.

cli-compression.name: CLI: Compression
cli-compression.description: Configure and test prompt compression from the CLI. Manage RTK filters, Caveman rules, stacked compression modes, and preview compression output with real prompts.

cli-contexts.name: CLI: Contexts & Sessions
cli-contexts.description: Manage context engineering configurations, RTK filter sets, and conversation sessions from the CLI. Apply context-relay settings and inspect active context pipelines.

cli-cost-usage.name: CLI: Cost & Usage
cli-cost-usage.description: View cost breakdowns, token usage, and call logs from the CLI. Filter by provider, model, or date range. Export usage reports and inspect per-connection spending.

cli-mcp.name: CLI: MCP
cli-mcp.description: Inspect the MCP server status, list registered tools and scopes, run tool invocations, and manage MCP audit logs from the CLI.

cli-a2a.name: CLI: A2A Protocol
cli-a2a.description: Interact with the OmniRoute A2A server from the CLI. Send tasks, inspect skill execution history, and test the JSON-RPC 2.0 agent-to-agent protocol interactively.

cli-tunnel.name: CLI: Tunnels
cli-tunnel.description: Start and stop tunnel connections (ngrok, Cloudflare, custom) from the CLI. Inspect active tunnel URLs, configure authentication, and test external reachability.

cli-backup-sync.name: CLI: Backup & Sync
cli-backup-sync.description: Backup and restore OmniRoute data from the CLI. Trigger incremental snapshots, sync to cloud storage, manage backup schedules, and restore from archive files.

cli-policy-audit.name: CLI: Policy & Audit
cli-policy-audit.description: Inspect audit logs, manage access policies, view telemetry data, and review request history from the CLI. Filter by event type, user, or time range for compliance workflows.

cli-batches.name: CLI: Batches & Files
cli-batches.description: Submit and monitor batch inference jobs from the CLI. Upload and manage files for batch processing, retrieve results, and integrate batch pipelines with CI/CD workflows.

cli-eval.name: CLI: Evals
cli-eval.description: Create and run evaluation suites, watch live benchmark progress, view scorecards, compare model performance, and integrate eval runs with CI workflows from the CLI.

cli-plugins-skills.name: CLI: Plugins, Skills & Memory
cli-plugins-skills.description: Manage Omni Skills (list, install, test, remove), plugins (create, configure), and persistent memory (search, add, clear) from the CLI.

cli-setup.name: CLI: Setup & Config
cli-setup.description: Run initial setup, configure global CLI settings, manage environment variables, check for updates, and configure autostart via the CLI setup and config commands.

cli-skill-collector.name: CLI: Agent Skill Collector
cli-skill-collector.description: Detect installed CLI coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Cline and more), search GitHub for matching agent skills, and install them to the detected tools via OmniRoute's built-in APIs.

config-codex-cli.name: Config: Codex CLI
config-codex-cli.description: Step-by-step agent workflow to configure the OpenAI Codex CLI on any machine (Linux, macOS, Windows) to use OmniRoute as an OpenAI-compatible backend. Detects OS and shell, writes config.toml and 7 named profiles, sets environment variables, and verifies the setup.

omni-github-skills.name: GitHub Skill Discovery
omni-github-skills.description: Search, score, scan, and import agent skills from GitHub repositories that contain SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, and similar agent skill files. Discover community skills across 160+ provider categories, evaluate relevance with heuristic scoring, check for malware or hardcoded secrets, and install into Hermes, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, or OpenCode agent directories.

featureFlags.title: Feature Flags
featureFlags.restartRequiredCount: {count, plural, one {# change requires} other {# changes require}} a server restart to take effect.
featureFlags.restartRequiredDescription: These flags only apply after the process reloads. Restart now or continue editing — pending flags stay queued until you confirm.
featureFlags.restartViewDescription: These flags only take effect after the server restarts. Toggle them like any other flag — the change is persisted immediately, but the new value is only read at process startup. Use the Restart Server banner above to apply.

REQUIRE_API_KEY.description: Require an API key for all incoming requests.

INPUT_SANITIZER_ENABLED.description: Enable input sanitization for all requests.

PII_REDACTION_ENABLED.description: Redact personally identifiable information from requests.

PII_RESPONSE_SANITIZATION.description: Sanitize personally identifiable information in provider responses.

PII_RESPONSE_SANITIZATION_MODE.description: Choose how response PII is handled: redact replaces it, warn only logs it, block rejects the response, and off disables sanitization.

OUTBOUND_SSRF_GUARD_ENABLED.description: Block outbound requests to private or internal IP ranges.

ALLOW_API_KEY_REVEAL.description: Allow authenticated dashboard users to reveal stored API keys instead of seeing masked values only.

PROXY_AUTO_SELECT_ENABLED.description: When no proxy is assigned to a connection, automatically select the first working registry proxy. Off by default because otherwise one registry proxy becomes a global fallback for all traffic (#3332).

OMNIROUTE_CONTROL_PLANE_PROXY_DIRECT_FAL.description: Allow OAuth and provider-validation flows to bypass a pinned proxy when proxy reachability checks fail. Off by default because this can change the account egress IP.

MITM_DISABLE_TLS_VERIFY.description: Disable TLS certificate verification for the MITM proxy.

OMNIROUTE_ALLOW_PRIVATE_PROVIDER_URLS.description: Allow provider URLs that point to private or internal networks.

OMNIROUTE_ALLOW_LOCAL_PROVIDER_URLS.description: Allow providers on localhost, LAN, and private IP ranges. This is required for local OpenAI-compatible models and enabled by default. Cloud-metadata endpoints such as 169.254.169.254 remain blocked.

TOOL_POLICY_MODE.description: Set the tool-use policy enforcement mode.

RATE_LIMIT_AUTO_ENABLE.description: Automatically enable rate limiting based on usage patterns.

ALLOW_MULTI_CONNECTIONS_PER_COMPAT_NODE.description: Allow multiple connections for each compatibility node.

RESPONSES_PASSTHROUGH_DROP_COMMENTARY.description: Remove internal commentary-phase output items from Responses API passthrough streams before forwarding them to clients. Disable this flag to receive raw upstream commentary.

OMNIROUTE_MCP_ENFORCE_SCOPES.description: Enforce scope restrictions for MCP tool access.

OMNIROUTE_MCP_COMPRESS_DESCRIPTIONS.description: Compress MCP tool descriptions to reduce token usage.

OMNIROUTE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_SERVICES.description: Disable all background services, including quota refresh and synchronization.

OMNIROUTE_RTK_TRUST_PROJECT_FILTERS.description: Trust project-level RTK filters without validation.

OMNIROUTE_ENABLE_LIVE_WS.description: Start the real-time dashboard WebSocket server on import at loopback port 20132. Set the flag to 0 or false to disable it. LAN exposure also requires LIVE_WS_HOST=0.0.0.0 and LIVE_WS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS.

OMNIROUTE_CODEX_WS_ENABLED.description: Allow Codex to use Responses over WebSocket. When disabled, Codex falls back to HTTP Responses.

OMNIROUTE_EMERGENCY_FALLBACK.description: Route budget-exhausted requests to the emergency free fallback provider and model.

STREAM_RECOVERY_ENABLED.description: Retry truncated upstream SSE streams transparently before any response bytes reach the client.

STREAM_RECOVERY_MIDSTREAM_ENABLED.description: Allow stream recovery to request the response again and stitch it after bytes have already reached the client.

MODEL_CATALOG_INCLUDE_NAMES.description: Include display-friendly name fields in /v1/models responses. Disable this for clients that accept model IDs only.

MODELS_CATALOG_PREFIX_MODE.description: Control model-ID prefixes in /v1/models: dual emits alias and canonical prefixes, alias emits short prefixes only, and canonical emits full provider IDs only.

ARENA_ELO_SYNC_ENABLED.description: Periodically synchronize Arena AI leaderboard ELO data for model-intelligence rankings.

CLI_COMPAT_ALL.description: Enable compatibility mode for all CLI clients.

PRICING_SYNC_ENABLED.description: Synchronize pricing data automatically. The PRICING_SYNC_ENABLED environment variable must also be true.

OMNIROUTE_AUTO_SYNC_CODEX_PROFILES.description: After provider-model synchronization, regenerate ~/.codex/*.config.toml profiles from the live catalog. This never changes the active or default Codex configuration and is off by default.

OMNIROUTE_AUTO_SYNC_CLAUDE_PROFILES.description: After provider-model synchronization, regenerate ~/.claude/profiles/<name>/settings.json profiles from the live catalog. This never changes the active or default Claude configuration and is off by default.

OMNIROUTE_DISABLE_LOCAL_HEALTHCHECK.description: Disable the local instance health-check endpoint.

SKILLS_SANDBOX_NETWORK_ENABLED.description: Enable network access in the skills sandbox.

issue.cooldownRecommendation: Wait for the upstream retry window or clear the cooldown after validating recovery.
issue.staleErrorRecommendation: Clear stale error fields so the connection is eligible for normal routing again.
issue.inactiveRecommendation: Reactivate only if this was not intentionally disabled.
